workflow_opportunities.title: Follow-up Email Drafting for Workshop Participants
workflow_opportunities.canonical_title: Follow-up email drafting for program participants
workflow_opportunities.normalized_key: follow up email drafting program participants
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Staff manually draft individual follow-up emails for each workshop participant (20+ per workshop). Takes 2-3 hours per workshop cycle.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Repetitive manual drafting of similar follow-up emails after each workshop session
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: AI-assisted template drafting with personalization fields for participant name, session date, resources, and next steps
human_review_points: All AI-generated content must be reviewed by program lead before sending
Community context and sensitive participant situations require human judgment
risks_or_exceptions: AI draft may sound too generic for participants with specific needs
Participant privacy information must be handled carefully
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 2-3 hours per workshop cycle to 30 minutes of review time
next_discovery_questions: Which workshop types have the most consistent follow-up patterns?
What personalization fields do staff need?
How do staff currently handle exceptions?
evidence_excerpts: Staff spend significant time preparing follow-up emails after each workshop session
Post-workshop follow-up messages are repetitive and time-consuming
Program follow-up emails could use approved templates and human review
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Program lead review required for all AI-generated content before sending
known_failure_cases: Generic-sounding messages may lose personal connection
Outdated program dates or resource links may be included

workflow_opportunities.title: Grant Report Data Compilation and Drafting
workflow_opportunities.canonical_title: Grant report data compilation and drafting
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Staff manually compile data from three separate spreadsheets (registrations, attendance, surveys) into grant reports. Takes approximately 5 hours per quarter.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Manual data compilation from disconnected spreadsheets for quarterly grant reports
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Structured data collection with AI-assisted report drafting from standardized inputs
human_review_points: AI-generated narrative must be reviewed for accuracy and context
Privacy requirements must be met when reporting participant data
risks_or_exceptions: AI transcription errors could introduce data inaccuracies
Qualitative program impact may be missed in structured data
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 5 hours per quarter to 1-2 hours of review and validation
next_discovery_questions: What metrics are most commonly requested by funders?
Can qualitative outcomes be captured in structured format?
What privacy rules apply to participant data?
evidence_excerpts: Program coordinator spends approximately 5 hours per quarter compiling grant reports from three separate spreadsheets
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Program manager review required for narrative accuracy and context
known_failure_cases: AI may miss important qualitative context not captured in structured data fields

workflow_opportunities.title: Workshop Attendance Data Analysis
workflow_opportunities.canonical_title: Workshop attendance data analysis for program planning
workflow_opportunities.normalized_key: workshop attendance data analysis program planning
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Past workshop attendance data is in a spreadsheet and requires manual analysis to inform content planning for future sessions.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: No automated way to analyze past attendance patterns to plan appropriate content levels
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: AI-assisted data analysis to identify attendance patterns and suggest content levels for different audience segments
human_review_points: Content recommendations must be reviewed by program lead who knows audience needs
risks_or_exceptions: Data may not capture all relevant factors affecting attendance
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces manual analysis time and improves data-informed program planning
next_discovery_questions: What attendance patterns would be most useful for planning?
How is attendance data currently structured?
What else affects content level decisions beyond attendance data?
evidence_excerpts: Past workshop attendance data could help plan appropriate content levels but requires manual analysis
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Program lead review of data-driven recommendations

workflow_opportunities.title: Volunteer Schedule Matching and Communication
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Volunteer scheduling managed through shared spreadsheet. Coordinator spends 4-6 hours per week on schedule matching and email confirmations.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Manual schedule matching and individual confirmation emails for 40+ active volunteers
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: AI-assisted schedule matching based on availability, skills, and program needs with automated confirmation draft generation
human_review_points: Schedule changes and volunteer preference exceptions require human handling
risks_or_exceptions: Some volunteers may struggle with technology changes
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 4-6 hours per week to 1-2 hours of review and exception handling
next_discovery_questions: What percentage of scheduling is routine vs exception?
How technology-comfortable are the volunteers?
What information is needed for effective schedule matching?
evidence_excerpts: Coordinator spends 4-6 hours per week on schedule matching and sending individual emails
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Coordinator review for schedule exceptions and volunteer preferences
known_failure_cases: Volunteers may find automated scheduling difficult to adapt to

workflow_opportunities.title: Donation Intake Logging and Receipt Generation
workflow_opportunities.canonical_title: Donation intake logging and receipt generation
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Donations logged manually into a tracking spreadsheet. Staff spend 8-10 hours per week on data entry and receipt generation.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Time-consuming manual data entry for donation tracking and tax receipt lookups
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Structured donation intake form with automated logging and receipt generation, OCR for handwritten donation notes
human_review_points: Food quality notes and special handling requirements need human review
risks_or_exceptions: OCR may inaccurately capture handwritten donation details
Food quality context may be lost in structured fields
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 8-10 hours per week to 2-3 hours of review and handling exceptions
next_discovery_questions: What donation information is most commonly needed for receipts?
How are food quality notes currently captured?
What are the most common data entry errors?
evidence_excerpts: Staff estimate 8-10 hours per week on donation data entry and receipt generation
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Staff review for food quality context and special handling notes
known_failure_cases: OCR may inaccurately capture handwritten information
Structured fields may miss important food quality context

workflow_opportunities.title: Multi-Funder Grant Report Compilation
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Data extracted manually from donation logs, volunteer hours, and attendance records for each funder's specific format. 6-8 hours per quarter per grant.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Each funder requires different metrics and formats, requiring manual data extraction from multiple sources
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Standardized data collection with AI-assisted report drafting that adapts content to each funder's required format and metrics
human_review_points: Program impact context requires human narrative input that structured data may not capture
risks_or_exceptions: AI may miss important program impact context not in structured data
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces compilation time while improving consistency across funder reports
next_discovery_questions: What common metrics exist across funders?
Can qualitative impact data be captured structurally?
What is the biggest time sink in current reporting?
evidence_excerpts: Program manager estimates 6-8 hours per quarter on each grant report
Each funder requires different metrics and reporting formats
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Program manager review for narrative context and impact description
known_failure_cases: Automation may miss important qualitative program impact context

workflow_opportunities.title: Founder Intake Information Consolidation
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Founder information comes through web forms, emails, and in-person conversations. Coordinator manually enters and reconciles data from all sources.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Founder intake data scattered across multiple sources requiring manual reconciliation
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: AI-assisted intake information extraction and consolidation from multiple sources into structured founder profiles
human_review_points: Founder readiness assessment and nuanced challenges require human judgment
risks_or_exceptions: AI may miss important nuances about founder readiness or specific challenges
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 3-4 hours per week of reconciliation to 30 minutes of review
next_discovery_questions: What information is most commonly duplicated across sources?
What founder readiness signals are hardest to capture?
evidence_excerpts: Coordinator spends 3-4 hours per week reconciling intake information from multiple sources
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Coordinator review for nuanced founder context
known_failure_cases: AI may miss subtle signals about founder readiness that are apparent in conversation

workflow_opportunities.title: Structured Mentor Matching with Profiles
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Mentors matched to founders based on hub director's personal knowledge. No structured database or criteria. Matching slows when director is unavailable.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Mentor matching depends on one person's knowledge with no structured backup
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: AI-assisted matching based on structured mentor profiles (industry, expertise, availability) and founder needs
human_review_points: Match quality and relationship dynamics require human judgment that AI cannot assess
risks_or_exceptions: AI matching may miss important interpersonal dynamics that affect mentorship success
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces dependency on single person's knowledge and speeds initial matching
next_discovery_questions: What criteria define a successful mentorship match?
How should mentor profiles be structured?
What is the current match success rate?
evidence_excerpts: Mentor matching relies on director's personal knowledge with no structured backup system
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Director review of AI-suggested matches before confirmation
known_failure_cases: AI may suggest matches that look good on paper but miss important interpersonal dynamics

workflow_opportunities.title: Post-Event Follow-up Email Generation
workflow_opportunities.normalized_key: post event follow up email generation
workflow_opportunities.current_process: After each event, coordinator drafts individual follow-up emails for 20-100 attendees. Takes 1-2 days per event.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Time-consuming individual email drafting after each event for large attendee lists
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Template-based follow-up generation with event-specific content and attendee personalization
human_review_points: Experienced staff must review event communications before sending
Personalization must not sound impersonal
risks_or_exceptions: Generic-sounding follow-ups may reduce attendee engagement
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 1-2 days per event to 1-2 hours of template customization and review
next_discovery_questions: What follow-up content categories are most common?
Which events have the most standardized follow-up patterns?
evidence_excerpts: Coordinator spends 1-2 days per event composing individual follow-up emails
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Experienced staff review all event communications before sending
known_failure_cases: Templated messages may feel impersonal if not properly customized

workflow_opportunities.title: Grant Opportunity Monitoring and Matching
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Staff manually scan funding websites, check eligibility, and match founders to opportunities. Estimated 5 hours per week.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Time-intensive manual research to find and match relevant grant opportunities for founders
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Automated grant opportunity monitoring with eligibility matching and deadline tracking. Browser automation for website monitoring.
human_review_points: Grant eligibility nuances and founder-fit assessment require human judgment
risks_or_exceptions: Browser automation may break when website layouts change
AI may miss subtle eligibility requirements
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 5 hours per week of scanning to 1 hour of review and matching refinement
next_discovery_questions: Which funding sources are most frequently used?
What eligibility criteria are most commonly checked?
evidence_excerpts: Staff member estimates spending 5 hours per week scanning funding websites and checking eligibility
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Staff review of AI-matched opportunities before presenting to founders
known_failure_cases: Browser automation may fail on website layout changes
AI may not catch subtle eligibility exceptions

workflow_opportunities.title: Workshop Scheduling Coordination
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Workshop scheduling involves email chains with multiple artists, venue calendar checks, and manual confirmations. 3-4 hours per workshop series.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Coordination-heavy manual scheduling with multiple stakeholders
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: AI-assisted scheduling that checks artist availability, venue calendar, and program themes against past patterns
human_review_points: Artist relationships and preferences require human judgment that AI cannot assess
risks_or_exceptions: AI scheduling may not account for artist relationship nuances and personal preferences
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 3-4 hours per series to 1 hour of review and exception handling
next_discovery_questions: What scheduling decisions are routine vs relationship-dependent?
How are artist preferences currently tracked?
evidence_excerpts: Staff estimate 3-4 hours per workshop series on scheduling coordination alone
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Program lead review for artist relationship considerations
known_failure_cases: AI may not understand artist availability nuances and relationship dynamics

workflow_opportunities.title: Artist Communication Template System
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Artist outreach, contracts, and coordination handled through individual emails. No standard templates. 5-6 hours per week on email with 30 artists.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: High-volume email communication with no templates for common scenarios
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Flexible communication templates for common scenarios (exhibition confirmations, payment notifications, call-for-submissions) with personalization fields
human_review_points: Personal artist relationships require human tone judgment
Experienced staff knowledge must be preserved in templates
risks_or_exceptions: Templated communications may feel impersonal to artists who value direct relationships
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 5-6 hours per week to 2-3 hours of personalization and review
next_discovery_questions: What are the most common communication scenarios?
Which templates would benefit most from AI assistance?
evidence_excerpts: Team spends 5-6 hours per week on email communication with approximately 30 active artists
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Experienced staff review for tone and relationship appropriateness
known_failure_cases: Templated messages may feel impersonal to artists

workflow_opportunities.title: Grant Application Content Reuse System
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Grant applications require rewriting content from past successful grants to match new funder priorities. 8-10 hours per application, 40% on adapting existing content.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Significant time spent reformatting and adapting content from previous successful grants
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Tagged content library with AI-assisted adaptation to match funder priorities and requirements
human_review_points: Narrative alignment with funder priorities requires human strategic judgment
risks_or_exceptions: AI adaptation may misrepresent organizational priorities or program details
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 8-10 hours per application to 4-5 hours of content selection and review
next_discovery_questions: What content sections are most commonly reused?
How do funder priorities change between applications?
evidence_excerpts: Staff estimate 40% of grant application time is spent formatting and adapting existing content
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Program lead review for narrative accuracy and funder alignment
known_failure_cases: AI may misrepresent organizational priorities when adapting content

workflow_opportunities.title: Newsletter Content Assembly and Drafting
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Content gathered from multiple staff via email and compiled by one editor. 6-8 hours per month on content gathering, formatting, and editing.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Manual content gathering from multiple contributors with significant editing time
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Structured content submission with AI-assisted drafting of recurring sections (program updates, artist spotlights, event listings)
human_review_points: Organizational voice and quality require human editorial review
risks_or_exceptions: AI-generated content may sound generic and lose organizational voice
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 6-8 hours per month to 3-4 hours of editorial review
next_discovery_questions: What newsletter sections have the most predictable content patterns?
How can organizational voice be preserved in templates?
evidence_excerpts: Editor estimates 6-8 hours per month on content gathering, formatting, and editing
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Editor review for voice, accuracy, and quality
known_failure_cases: AI-generated content may lose organizational voice and sound generic

workflow_opportunities.title: Youth Intake Transcription and Digitization
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Youth intake information collected on paper forms and transcribed to digital spreadsheet. 2-3 hours per week on data entry.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Manual transcription of paper intake forms prone to errors and time-consuming
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Digital intake form with structured fields and optional AI-assisted transcription from handwritten notes
human_review_points: Contextual notes written in margins of paper forms may be lost in digitization
risks_or_exceptions: OCR error risk for handwritten notes
Important contextual notes may be lost in structured fields
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 2-3 hours per week of transcription to 30 minutes of validation
next_discovery_questions: What intake fields are most essential for follow-up?
How are margin notes currently used?
evidence_excerpts: Staff spend 2-3 hours per week transcribing paper intake forms into digital spreadsheet
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Case worker review for contextual notes preservation
known_failure_cases: OCR may inaccurately capture handwritten margin notes that contain important context

workflow_opportunities.title: Employer Outreach Follow-up Tracking
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Employer contacts and outreach tracked in spreadsheet. Specialist sends individual follow-up emails manually. 3-4 hours per week for 50+ employer relationships.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: Manual follow-up tracking and email composition for a large employer network
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Automated check-in reminders with AI-assisted draft follow-up emails based on last interaction context
human_review_points: Personal employer relationships require careful tone management in AI-generated communications
risks_or_exceptions: Automated reminders may feel impersonal to long-standing employer partners
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces 3-4 hours per week to 1 hour of review and personalization
next_discovery_questions: What is the optimal check-in frequency for different employer types?
What employer information is most important for follow-up context?
evidence_excerpts: Specialist spends 3-4 hours per week on employer follow-up coordination with 50+ active relationships
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Specialist review for tone and relationship appropriateness
known_failure_cases: Automated follow-ups may feel impersonal to established employer partners

workflow_opportunities.title: Workshop Follow-up with Personalized Feedback
workflow_opportunities.current_process: Staff compose individual follow-up emails for 15-20 participants per workshop with resources, next steps, and feedback.
workflow_opportunities.pain_point: High-volume personalized follow-up for workshop participants is time-intensive
workflow_opportunities.possible_ai_support: Template-assisted follow-ups with structured personalization fields for participant-specific resources and feedback sections
human_review_points: Each participant has unique needs that templates must accommodate
AI-generated feedback may not be specific enough to be useful
risks_or_exceptions: AI-generated feedback may be too generic to be helpful for job seekers
workflow_opportunities.staff_impact: Reduces email composition time while maintaining personalization quality
next_discovery_questions: What personalization fields are most important for useful follow-ups?
How specific does feedback need to be to be helpful?
evidence_excerpts: Staff compose individual follow-up emails for 15-20 participants per workshop with resources and feedback
workflow_opportunities.required_human_review: Staff review for feedback specificity and accuracy
known_failure_cases: AI-generated feedback may be too generic to address individual participant needs

failure_cases.what_failed: AI-generated follow-up email may sound too generic for community context
failure_cases.why_it_failed: AI lacks understanding of the personal relationships staff build with program participants
failure_cases.missing_context: Participant-specific context and relationship history are not available to AI
failure_cases.human_review_required: Program lead must review all AI-generated communications before sending
failure_cases.suggested_prevention: Include participant context fields in templates and require human review for tone and personalization
evidence_excerpts: Staff worry automated messages may sound too generic and miss personal connection
Templated messages need to accommodate participant-specific situations
Staff emphasized human review for all external communications
failure_cases.normalized_key: ai generated follow up email may sound too generic for community context

failure_cases.what_failed: AI may introduce data errors when transcribing or compiling reporting data
failure_cases.why_it_failed: AI transcription may not accurately capture data from disconnected spreadsheet sources
failure_cases.missing_context: Data sources are not standardized and contain inconsistent formats
failure_cases.human_review_required: Program manager must verify all AI-compiled data before submission
failure_cases.suggested_prevention: Standardize data collection formats and implement validation checks before AI processing
evidence_excerpts: Staff concerned that automation may introduce errors in data transcription
failure_cases.normalized_key: ai may introduce data errors when transcribing or compiling reporting data

failure_cases.what_failed: Automated donation tracking may miss important food quality context
failure_cases.why_it_failed: Structured fields cannot capture nuanced quality observations that staff currently note
failure_cases.missing_context: Food quality notes and special handling requirements are context-dependent
failure_cases.human_review_required: Staff must review automated entries for quality and special handling context
failure_cases.suggested_prevention: Include free-text quality notes field and require staff sign-off on each donation entry
evidence_excerpts: Staff concerned that automating donation tracking may not capture important food quality context
failure_cases.normalized_key: automated donation tracking may miss important food quality context

failure_cases.what_failed: AI-generated client communications may use inappropriate language for vulnerable situations
failure_cases.why_it_failed: AI cannot fully understand the diverse needs and circumstances of clients in vulnerable situations
failure_cases.missing_context: Client vulnerability context and appropriate communication tone are not fully documented
failure_cases.human_review_required: Staff with client knowledge must review all AI-generated communications
failure_cases.suggested_prevention: Develop communication tone guidelines and require staff review before sending to clients
evidence_excerpts: Staff worry standardized templates may make communications feel impersonal for vulnerable clients
failure_cases.normalized_key: ai generated client communications may use inappropriate language for vulnerable situations

failure_cases.what_failed: Browser automation for grant website monitoring may break on layout changes
failure_cases.why_it_failed: Websites frequently update their layouts, breaking automated scraping or monitoring scripts
failure_cases.missing_context: AI/browser automation cannot predict or adapt to website layout changes without human intervention
failure_cases.human_review_required: Staff must periodically verify automation is still working correctly
failure_cases.suggested_prevention: Implement monitoring alerts for automation failures and maintain manual fallback process
evidence_excerpts: Browser automation may help monitor funding website updates but may break when layouts change
failure_cases.normalized_key: browser automation for grant website monitoring may break on layout changes

failure_cases.what_failed: AI-generated follow-up may overpromise services to founders
failure_cases.why_it_failed: AI lacks awareness of actual service capacity, eligibility criteria, and current availability
failure_cases.missing_context: Real-time capacity and eligibility status are not available to AI systems
failure_cases.human_review_required: Staff must review AI-generated commitments before communication with founders
failure_cases.suggested_prevention: Include capacity constraints in AI context and require staff approval before any service commitment communication
evidence_excerpts: Staff are worried that AI may overpromise services to founders
failure_cases.normalized_key: ai generated follow up may overpromise services to founders

failure_cases.what_failed: AI may not distinguish between public information and internal-only notes
failure_cases.why_it_failed: AI lacks context about what content is appropriate for public vs internal use
failure_cases.missing_context: Content classification rules and organizational privacy policies are not systematically documented
failure_cases.human_review_required: Staff must review AI-generated promotional content for public vs internal appropriateness
failure_cases.suggested_prevention: Define content classification guidelines and include in AI system context, require staff review for all external content
evidence_excerpts: Uncertainty about what information should be public vs internal-only when using AI to draft promotional content

failure_cases.why_it_failed: Handwritten margin notes contain nuanced observations that OCR may miss or misinterpret
failure_cases.missing_context: Case worker observations written in margins of intake forms are not structured
failure_cases.human_review_required: Case worker must review AI-transcribed intake notes for accuracy

adoption_risk_notes.suggested_mitigation: Set clear expectations that AI is for reducing repetitive work, not increasing output volume without recognition

adoption_risk_notes.description: Concern that digitizing intake may lose important contextual notes written in margins
adoption_risk_notes.suggested_mitigation: Design intake system that preserves free-text case worker observations alongside structured fields

adoption_risk_notes.description: AI-generated feedback may not be specific enough to be useful for job seekers
adoption_risk_notes.suggested_mitigation: Design feedback templates with structured specificity fields and require staff review for usefulness before sending

adoption_risk_notes.description: Privacy concerns when recording sensitive youth information in structured or AI-processed systems
